title: Gesneria neglecta
intro: Gesneria neglecta, also known as the "Pigeon Wings" or "Cuphea", is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Gesneria neglecta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gesneria neglecta is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture to treat respiratory conditions, and its leaves can also be consumed fresh or dried to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Gesneria neglecta is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, which is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the African violet or gesneriad family. The Gesneriaceae family belongs to the order Lamiales, which includes other flowering plants such as mint, honeysuckle, and figwort. The classification of Gesneria neglecta reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships to other plants within the Gesneriaceae family.
